sentences.text: Hey, how's it going, Jared?
Good, Nathan.
How are you?
I'm doing well, doing well, can't complain.
Have you had a good week so far?
Yeah, so far, so good.
How about yourself?
Yeah, it's been good.
We, we had a work event in Chicago the past couple of days, so… I got home yesterday afternoon, but everything's good.
It's been a good week.
And where… and where are you located?
Where do you live?
I'm in Nashville, Tennessee.
A city I've never been to.
Where… where are you right now?
Sioux Falls, South Dakota.
Okay, I've never been there either.
Well, I'll need to… I certainly have made it a point to visit Nashville at some point, and welcome you to Sioux Falls, too.
It's a beautiful city.
Yeah, thank you.
I, I would love to come up there.
I've heard good things about it in terms of, like, the scenery, and there's, like, a lot of hiking trails and everything up there too, isn't there?
Yeah, there's lots to do outdoors.
Yeah, our big season's coming up with all the hunters get out and kill those pheasants, so… Yep.
Is that, do you like doing that?
Do you get out there?
I, myself, no, I've never gotten into it.
It was just my family was raised in the, you know, in the city, and my dad was never into it, and I never got into it, but I'm one of the very few It's very, it's very popular.

You know what, that's how I am down here, it's like whitetail deer, and… My dad never was into it, and I just never got into it, but everybody I went to school with was like, oh, let's go hunting, and I'm like, … I'm okay.
Other hobbies, other interests, yeah.
Yeah, yeah, exactly, so… Well, thank you for hopping on, man.
I'm excited to talk to you.
I know… I think Ashley introduced us?
Yes, yep.
So, I've been working with Ashley now for over the past year.
… she's had some success with her portfolio, so I'm assuming that's why she… she intro'd us.
So I'm excited to, you know, learn a little bit more about your portfolio, and show with you in detail everything that we offer.
Yeah, well, your learner curve will be, painless, because I essentially have been assigned and taken over all of Ashley's properties, and so the property list isn't changing, with a few exceptions.
And yeah, I guess this morning, I was just interested to learn from you.
Good morning, Ashley.
Hi, guys.
Finally.
No.
Learn from you on, what Rentable can offer, so… Okay.
Yeah, no, happy to do that.
You say you're taking over all, I guess, what, 13 to 17 of Ashley's properties?
That… those numbers sound right.
Somewhere… somewhere in that neighborhood.
Okay.
Yeah.
Awesome.
Well, Ashley, where are you going?
So, I'm keeping 5 to self-manage, and then I will help with some of the internal, like, training, development, software pieces that we're working on.
A little bit of a different role change.
Gotcha, okay, fair enough, fair enough.
So yeah, no, happy to, happy to share, … Yeah, I'll dive in.
Yeah.
Everything that you're getting right now with us.
… let me go to my screen here.
Can you see this okay?
Yes.
Awesome.
So are you guys, just so I can, make sure I'm explaining everything right, are you guys still using Apartments.
com and Zillow, or did you drop Apartments.
com?
So previously I had dropped all apartments.
com in my region, and I was in the process of dropping all Zillow again, just to maintain rentable.
It just is so much more cost-effective.
Absolutely.
Okay, cool.
That's, that's what I needed to know.
So, in terms of everything we're doing, Jared, we're an ILS, just like an Apartments.
com or a Zillow, but way more cost-effective, as Ashley just kind of alluded to.
So what we're offering is… I think right now, the way we have it set up, because you guys are still using Boston Post for your property management software, is that right?
Yes.
Okay, I… I believe the way we did it was we built, like, a custom… we either did a custom feed, or we're updating everything manually for you guys.
… I honestly can't remember.
I think Carly knows a little more about that than I do.
But whatever's being done, it's already being done, so you don't have to worry too much on your end, Jared.

But what we're doing is we're advertising your properties on our rentable site, and then from there, we're syndicating it out to our massive syndicate network, which includes basically everywhere except for Apartments.
com.
And then we have an AI tool working in the background, so, like, if your properties are utilizing Zillow, we're not gonna push and duplicate listings there.
But if you ended up dropping it, or on the properties that's not utilizing Zillow, we'll still push and get you over there for those properties as well.
So, we're basically getting you everywhere for a fraction of the cost of any of the other ILSs, and then… On top of that, we have an all-inclusive targeted social ad piece.
So…
anytime somebody is going on a search engine and saying, show me apartments in Sioux Falls.
The next time that they get on their Facebook or Instagram feed, within, I'd say, like, 30 or 45 seconds of scrolling, we're gonna hit them with a targeted rentable ad that looks like this.
It'll have pictures of your individual property, some details about it, whether it's pet-friendly, or in-unit laundry, whatever it may be.

And then, where our ads really differ from a lot of other companies is a lot of companies will redirect that person to the property website, or even worse, they'll… Redirect them to their website, where they can then see all the other ads that they're… they're doing for other companies.
We do neither of those.
So, these are lead generating, so anytime somebody clicks Learn More, we're gonna have them fill out a guest card form.
We'll capture their names, their emails, their phone numbers.
And then we'll deliver it to you as a guest card.
And I believe we're sending leads to an on-site leasing email right now.
Is that right, Ashley?
Yep, so each… all leads go to the property emails.
Okay.
So… All leads directly to the property emails.
… Perfect.
So, and that's essentially rentable.
How is the rentable… you have an image of a phone with, scrolling on Facebook.
Does a prospect who's seeking… I mean, how do you capture that qualified prospect through, like, Facebook as an example?
Yeah, so the way we're kind of, like, targeting that person is if they're searching for it and looking for a community on any search engine, like Yahoo, Google, Bing, whatever it may be.
When they click on the ad, so, like, within this ad, they can scroll and see multiple photos of your property.
And you see the Learn More?

Once they click Learn More on something, or try to click into it, it's gonna pop up a form requiring… asking them to put in their names, their emails, their phone numbers, and then it would send to you as a guest card.
So that's how we're capturing that lead.
How did I introduce you?
how is our, Costello properties being targeted to the audience that you're describing?
Because, I mean, it is a possibility if someone's, like, did a search for apartments in Sioux Falls,
Is it… like, how is that… methodology to determine on what lead is being sent to the prospects looking?
Because it could… Very likely not be one of our leads, correct?
One of our property leads.
Correct?
Any… are you talking about if they get sent from this… this social ad?
Yeah, or if they just did a self-guided, like, search in Google Apartments in Sioux Falls, is that… In what order does Rentable deliver them?
a property listing, is it based on what that… Property companies paying to be, like.
At the top of the list of those Being sent to those prospects.
I guess I'm confused on what you're asking.
So, like, every lead that we send in, whether it's from our site, from our social ads, or from our syndicate network, it all gets sent to you as a rentable lead and attributed to us.
Unless you have the direct agreement.
with a different source.
like Zillow and Apartments.
com.
Yeah.
Very, yeah.
So, like, if you were to go to Zillow, right, and you were to go on there, search, you say, hey, I want to learn more, you fill out the information, it sends it.
For if you have a direct agreement with Zillow, it would show up in your system as a Zillow lead.
Same thing with us, even if it's on one of our syndicate sources, though, because our syndicate site basically sends it to us for us to send to you.
So, all of the leads that come in from us are attributed to us.
Okay.
Does that answer your question?
No, I understand what you just….
What yours?
I guess my question is, is, let me try to ask this differently.
… I, myself, am looking for an apartment in Sioux Falls, and I just googled, apartments available in Sioux Falls in this rent range.
I've kind of created, like, … A stamp of… search history, and so next time I go to Facebook, they may understand that… that algorithm or whatever, and I'm looking for an apartment in Sioux Falls.

There's a possibility, as I scroll through my Facebook feeds, that I may get a listing, a promoted page sent to me, but not necessarily… it could be one of our… it could be one of Costello's competing management companies.
Am I understanding that correctly?
Yeah, yeah, it could, if they're running social ads with us.
Right.
So… so, I mean, it really depends on, like, the criterias, right?
So, if somebody's searching longer-tail keywords, saying, you know, show me two bedrooms.
the other place may not have two bedrooms, or two bedrooms available.
Or maybe they're searching for an affordable community, whereas this one is, like, listed as an affordable community.
So different things are gonna stand out.
We also have, like, a mile radius.
So, like, if you're in… it's like a 25 to 40 mile radius, … so, like, if you're in Sioux Falls, you're not gonna pick up somebody looking for an apartment up in North Dakota.

like, if somebody's… what I… sorry, let me… let me make this make more… so, I don't know several cities around Sioux Falls, so let me, … Let me look this up on the map real quick, so I can talk in terms of, like, your language.
So, for an example, like, if… since it's… it's got, like, such a radius, like, you're not going to… If you've got somebody looking for a property out in Worthington, South Dakota.
like, you're not gonna have an apartment in Sioux Falls show up for them, because that… it looks like it's over 25 to 40 miles away.
Right.
Does that make sense?
Yes, yep.
And it's really just going to… like, we use AI to make sure that we're getting, like, the best results when it comes to these social ads.
So, like.
it really depends, like you said, on the criteria you're searching for.
I'm not gonna guarantee that, you know, one of your competitors that uses us down the road doesn't have their property show up first.
before yours, like, I can't really guarantee that, … it really goes off, like you said, Facebook's algorithm.
But if somebody goes on Google and says, show me apartments in Sioux Falls, it's gonna usually be an Apartments.
com-sponsored ad that nobody else can beat.

And then from there on, it's gonna be a bunch of different ILSs where they can click into, and then you can find your property within that ILS within the first However many seconds of scrolling and looking.
Okay.
Wow.
These are just, like, retargeted ads.
So, I mean… Yeah.
I mean, that's the best way to put it.
It's just gonna be randomized and… and whatnot, you may see this same property show up twice within scrolling.
You might scroll past it, and then a few seconds later, you see another one for it.
So….
Another question I, … I wrote down, I have, is… Like myself, being in a supervisory role of the site managers that are accepting and managing those leads.
Is there reporting, that I have access to to see, like, the volume and… The conversion rates of… Myself, see how… see how the advertising's working for us, for each particular property?
Yeah, absolutely.
So, let's see here… share the screen so you can see it.
So…
So here, you would see, like, when you log in, you have access to see everything.
… you can see the property names, you can go in, you can edit them however you need.
If you needed it, Carly's your direct point of contact, so she's able to go in and update things like this for you.
But you can also go in and see analytics.
So, like, over the past 30 days so far, we've sent 87 leads, and then you can go in, you can see, okay, for Majestic Ridge, there was 5, Brandon Heights, there was 12, and so forth and so forth.
You can change the timeframe, so you can go back to the past 30 days.
I mean, sorry, 90 days, year, however long, 250 leads in the past 90 days.
You see Brandon Heights had 73, Majestic Ridge 15.
Out of these 73, 55 were emails, 18 were calls.
You can also go over here to the Leads tab on the side, and it'll show you the person's name, the email, what property it was to, and what they said, and the date and time they said it.
Same thing with calls.
You can go in here, you can see, okay, cool, this phone number's the one that called this apartment.
You can go in, you actually click play, and listen to what that conversation sounded like, too.
Have you found that to be useful, Ashley?
Very.
It is nice to be able to listen, especially the new hires, or if I'm having ongoing issues, I can hear the phone conversations, and I can see when they're answering their calls and when they're not.
Okay.
I'm actually in the middle of a meeting.
Can you come back, or are you a current resident?
I am.
Okay, hold on one second.
I'll be right back, you guys.
Sounds good.
Now, Nathan, how… do I get set up for login credentials for… to review the reporting you just shared?
Yeah, it's very simple.
I can send it over, Carly can send it over, whichever, but we just send it over to our support team, and then they create the login for you on the back end.

Your username will end up being your email, and then typically they set it up to where your first password with us is just rentable one, and then it allows you to create whatever password you want from there.
Okay.
Yeah, I'd like, I'd like to, … be shared that so I could log in and kind of, you know, goof around and see, for my… play around in there myself.
… But for one of you brief, pages you showed, it looked pretty straightforward, which… user-friendly, I should say, which is good.
Okay, ….
It's pretty simplistic.

We, … that was, like, the main point when we started our company, is we wanted it to be easy for renters to find a place to live, and we wanted it to be easier on you guys for, you know, to go through our dashboard.
So….
I know we like easy.

Our site managers have a very tough job, and to simplify that process, which is one of our most important processes, if our prospects can find us, and call us and let's not seek them, that's, always… that's what we're hoping for.
… And I understand, I'm just kind of jumping into this, and Ashley, shared with me last week that I… Our Upwork… our contract is up for renewal, is that correct?
It is, yes.

And right now, I think your pricing… so… typically, we run $200 a month per property, and because Ashley brought on… Basically, her entire portfolio, we gave her a deal down to $75 a month per property.
Okay.
Does… as of we stand this morning, Thursday morning, does that still need to be signed?
That does, to my knowledge….
Question for Carly?
That is a question for Colleen.
It looks like in our system that it still needs to be signed.
Yeah, it hasn't been signed yet, and I guess it… if we're not doing advertising cost-effective, I… I'll have a separate conversation with my five properties.
Because I, I do want to keep rentable.
Okay.
Sweet.
Ashley, did you share Carly's email with me?
I, I don't….
Yeah, I can resend it.
Could you please, I don't know, I think… I thought you had, but I….
Yeah, nope, I can resend that over.
I can put it in here as well, if you need me to.
Maybe.
I feel like Zoom keeps updating… there it is, okay.
Zoom keeps, like, updating everything and moving, like, the icons around on the bottom, so it's getting harder to find things.
There, I just sent you what her email is.
Thank you.
Awful.
So yeah, do you have any other questions for me about anything?
Not at this time.
I'm sure I may for you as well, Ashley, as I dive in, log into… look into this a little bit further.
So, I'm looking forward to that, though.
And… I have your email address, of course I have yours, Ashley, and if I have questions, I'll let you know.
Okay, perfect, that sounds….
Thank you, guys.
Yeah, you're welcome.
Thank you.
Have a great day.
You too.
Thanks, see ya.
Bye.
